perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence at all times and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to kno w” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, all liability will be on the Party that is responsible. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confid ential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in a reasonable manner and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to know” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, the Party that is responsible will not be liable unless the information was divulged with malicious intent. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confidential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'strictest of confidence at all times' to 'reasonable manner' and adding a 'malicious intent' clause for liability introduces ambiguity regarding the standard of care required. This contradicts the original intent of Section V, which implied a high level of protection for confidential information. The original text implies a strict liability, whereas the modified version introduces a negligence standard which makes it vague on how Confidential Information will be maintained. The ambiguity can cause dispute, especially if other sections of the agreement refer to the original, strict definition of maintaining Confidential Information for enforcement or remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'strictest of confidence at all times', implying strict liability. The changed text weakens this to a 'reasonable manner' and adds a 'malicious intent' requirement for liability. This introduces ambiguity and a negligence standard, conflicting with the contradicted text, which states that 'any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm' and entitles the harmed party to 'all remedies available at law'. The modified section significantly reduces the liability for breaches, even non-malicious ones, creating a direct contradiction regarding the enforceability and remedies outlined in the contradicted text. The change alters the standard of care required and the circumstances under which liability is triggered, making the two sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until such information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.changed_text: The bounded Party’s(ies’) duty to hold the Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the date of this agreement, regardless of whether such information qualifies as a trade secret or written notice is given releasing such Party from this Agreem ent.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section VI states that confidentiality lasts until the information is no longer a trade secret or written notice is given. The modified version limits this to two years, regardless of its trade secret status. This creates a direct contradiction. Even if the information remains a trade secret, the obligation to keep it confidential expires after two years per the updated text. This modifies the time period, because there is no more need to have the consideration if the information still qualifies as a trade secret, which causes an inconsistency in the text. The contradiction can cause dispute, especially if other sections of the agreement refer to the original term of maintaining Confidential Information for enforcement or remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the ter m “Confidential Information” shall ONLY include documents, records, information and data that are clearly marked as 'Confidential' or 'Proprietary' in written format. Confidential Information also includes any and all, work products, studies and other material prepared by or in the possession or contro l of the other Party, which contain, include, refer to or otherwise reflect or are generated from any Confidential Information. However, Confidential Information does not include: (a) information generally available to the public; (b) widely used pro gramming practices or algorithms; (c) information rightfully in the possession of the Parties prior to signing this Agreement; and (d) information independently developed without the use of any of the provided Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states confidentiality lasts until the information is no longer a trade secret or written notice is given. The changed text limits this to two years, regardless of its trade secret status. This directly contradicts the implied indefinite nature of 'Confidential Information' as defined in the Contradicted Text, which does not specify a time limit. The change introduces a temporal limitation where none existed before, creating a conflict. Therefore, the change causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the general understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter and does not necessarily supersede all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings, particularly those documented in side letters or contemporaneous communications.
perturbation.explanation: The original integration clause (Section VII) states this agreement is the complete understanding and supersedes all prior agreements. The modified version weakens this, stating it's a 'general' understanding and doesn't necessarily supersede prior agreements, especially those in 'side letters'. This introduces significant ambiguity. It becomes unclear whether side letters or other communications are part of the binding agreement or not. This contradicts the intention of a standard integration clause and undermines the certainty of the contract. The ambiguity can cause dispute, especially if other sections of the agreement refer to the original integrated format for enforcement or remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed under the laws in the State of Florida . IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Agreement as of the date written below. 1st Party’s Signature ______________________________ Date _________________ Print Name ______________________________ 2nd Party’s Signature ______________________________ Date _________________ Print Name ______________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text established the agreement as the complete and superseding understanding. The changed text weakens this, stating it's a 'general' understanding and doesn't necessarily supersede prior agreements, especially those in 'side letters'. This introduces ambiguity, and the explanation mentions that this contradicts the intention of a standard integration clause and undermines the certainty of the contract. While the contradicted text (signatures and governing law) doesn't directly clash with the language about side letters, the change in the integration clause introduces significant uncertainty about what constitutes the full agreement, which is essential for the enforceability and interpretation of the entire document, including the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: If a court finds that any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall be interpreted so as best to affect the intent of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: If a court finds that any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Agreement shall be interpreted according to the court's discretion and may not necessarily reflect the original intent of the Parties. In such cases, the Parties agree to renegotiate the affected provision in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: The original severability clause (Section VIII) states the remaining agreement should be interpreted to best reflect the Parties' intent. The modified version says interpretation is at the court's discretion and may not reflect the Parties' intent. This introduces ambiguity and undermines the purpose of a severability clause, which is to preserve as much of the original agreement as possible. The forced renegotiation also adds uncertainty. The ambiguity can cause dispute, especially if other sections of the agreement refer to the original intents of the format for enforcement or remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the general understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter and does not necessarily supersede all prior proposals, agreements, representations, and understandings, particularly those documented in side letters or contemporaneous communications.
perturbation.justification: The original severability clause aimed to uphold the parties' intent as much as possible. The changed version permits the court to disregard the parties' intent, directly conflicting with the contradicted text stating the agreement expresses the Parties' understanding. This is a clear contradiction because one clause prioritizes the Parties' intent, while the other allows it to be superseded, creating ambiguity and undermining the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information, any breach of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm for which damages and/or equitable relief may be sought. The harmed Party in this Agreement shall be entitled to all remedies available at law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that a breach of this Agreement may or may not cause irreparable harm, and the remedies available to the harmed Party shall be limited to actual monetary damages, not to exceed $1,000. Equitable relief is expressly waived.
perturbation.explanation: The original enforcement clause (Section IX) states a breach causes irreparable harm and allows all remedies at law. The modified version states a breach 'may or may not' cause irreparable harm, limits remedies to monetary damages (capped at $1,000), and waives equitable relief. This directly contradicts the original clause. It drastically reduces the protections for confidential information and limits the ability to seek meaningful recourse for breaches. The contradiction can cause dispute, especially if other sections of the agreement refer to the unique and sensitive nature of the Confidential Information for enforcement or remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of the Parties shall be to hold and maintain the Confidential Information in a reasonable manner and to their agents, employees, representatives, affiliates, and any other individual or entity that is on a “need to know” basis. If any such Confidential Information shall reach a third (3rd) party, or become public, the Party that is responsible will not be liable unless the information was divulged with malicious intent. Neither Party shall, without the written approval of the other Party, publish, copy, or use the Confidential Information for their sole benefit. If requested, either Party shall be bound to return any and all materials to the Requesting Party within ____ days. This Section shall not apply to the 1st Party if this Agreement is Unilateral as marked in Sect ion II.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and thus the contradicted text. The original enforcement clause stated that any breach causes irreparable harm and allows all remedies at law, while the modified version states a breach 'may or may not' cause irreparable harm, limits remedies to monetary damages (capped at $1,000), and waives equitable relief. This directly conflicts with the 'Confidential Information' clause, creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party concerning confidential information.
